fix(ghpr): parse Review.Author as nested object not string (#27)

* test(lifecycle): add table-driven tests for Manager state machine

Cover all key event transitions: EventCIFailed dispatch, max CI
attempts exhaustion (with DB log verification), EventReviewChanges,
EventPRMerged closes bead, EventPRConflicting logging, EventPRClosed
cleanup, and concurrent HandleEvent calls verified under -race.

Also fix a race condition in HandleEvent where PRState fields were
written outside the mutex — state mutations are now fully covered by
the lock, with logging deferred until after unlock.

Bead: Forge-r3p

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ghpr): parse Review.Author as nested object not string

GitHub's API returns the review author as {"login": "..."}
but Review.Author was declared as a plain string. This caused
json.Unmarshal to fail for any PR that had reviews, making
CheckStatus return an error and bellows silently skip those PRs.
As a result, EventReviewChanges was never emitted and no review
fix workers were ever dispatched.

Fix by introducing ReviewAuthor{Login string} and updating
Review.Author from string to ReviewAuthor.

* feat(reviewfix): log smith error details and thread resolution to DB

On Smith exit 1, log ResultSubtype / RateLimited flag / ErrorOutput (truncated)
to the events table as EventReviewFixSmithError so the root cause is visible
via 'forge history' and the DB without needing discarded stderr output.

After a successful review fix, log each resolved GitHub thread as
EventReviewThreadResolved with author and body snippet, so it is clear
which specific comments were marked resolved via the GraphQL
resolveReviewThread mutation.

* fix(reviewfix,cifix): use provider fallback when Claude is rate-limited

Both reviewfix.Fix and cifix.Fix were calling smith.Spawn which
hardcodes provider.Claude. When the Claude quota is exhausted the
workers immediately fail on both attempts with rate_limited.

Apply the same provider fallback loop used by the main pipeline:
- Add Providers []provider.Provider to FixParams (both packages)
- Try each provider in order; on rate-limit fall back to the next
- If ALL providers are rate-limited, abort immediately (don't waste attempts)
- Wire Providers: provider.FromConfig(...) in daemon.go for both callers

Ref PR #27 (fix/ghpr-review-author-unmarshal)

* fix: address PR #27 review comments

- Remove incorrect ExitCode==0 guard from RateLimited clear in cifix and
  reviewfix. Claude can exit 0 with is_error:true (subtype:success) when
  a session is rate-limit rejected, so only the ResultSubtype+IsError
  check is correct (matching the design intent documented in smith.go).

- Add TestReviewAuthorUnmarshal regression test in ghpr_test.go for the
  Review.Author nested-object unmarshal bug fixed in this PR.

---------

Co-authored-by: Robin Smith <Robin.Edvard.Smith@fhi.no>

commit.message:
Fix middleware MIDDLEWARE_INVOCATION_FAILED on Vercel

Three issues: public path check was happening after Supabase init (crash
risk on cold starts), matcher was missing _next/data/* paths (client-side
nav fetches were being intercepted and crashing), and no try/catch around
getSession() meant any auth error surfaced as a 500. Fixed all three.

commit.message:
Harden proxy and refactor config to map-keyed tagged enums (#29)

## Summary
- Hardened the proxy with request body limits (10 MiB), HTTP client
timeouts, and improved error forwarding
- Refactored strategy configuration from a flat struct to a
discriminated `StrategyConfig` enum, making invalid field combinations
unrepresentable at the type level
- Changed downstream config from `[[downstream]]` array-of-tables to
`[downstream.<name>]` map, eliminating the redundant `name` field and
making uniqueness implicit

## Key Changes

### Proxy hardening
- Added `DefaultBodyLimit::max(10 MiB)` to cap request sizes
- Configured `reqwest::Client` with explicit timeouts (10s connect, 600s
request)
- Improved error messages and logging in SSE/POST proxy handlers

### Strategy config refactor
- Replaced `Strategy` enum + flat `DownstreamConfig` with
`StrategyConfig` tagged enum using `#[serde(tag = "strategy", flatten)]`
  - `Passthrough { auth_hint }` — only passthrough-specific fields
- `ChainedOauth { oauth_authorize_url, oauth_token_url, ... }` — only
chained OAuth fields
- Updated all route handlers to pattern match on variants instead of
checking `ds.strategy == Strategy::*`
- Refactored `post_downstream_token()` to take explicit parameters
instead of full config struct

### Downstream config as TOML map
- Config format changed from `[[downstream]]` with `name = "foo"` to
`[downstream.foo]`
- Removed `name` field from `DownstreamConfig` — name comes from the map
key
- Removed separate `downstream_index` HashMap from `AppState` — lookups
go directly through `Config.downstream`

## Test plan
- [x] All 37 existing tests pass (unit + integration)
- [x] `cargo clippy` clean
- [x] New tests for tagged enum parsing and validation
- [x] Updated `config.example.toml` to reflect new format

https://claude.ai/code/session_017jYj7yjHfRkX9KhWWx6sNV

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: M18 — offline agent path for Gemini and Ollama

nexus agent run now works with Gemini and Ollama (no Anthropic key
required) via a structured-output single-prompt path:

- offline_agent_prompt() in ai.py builds a JSON-schema prompt that
  injects the full project snapshot (stats, tasks, stale, ready work)
  and asks the model to return {"observations": [...], "actions": [...]}
- agent_run routes to _run_offline_agent() when not ai.supports_tools;
  the Anthropic tool-use path is completely unchanged
- _build_offline_context() assembles project data (capped at 10 active
  tasks, 5 ready, stale/blocked lists) without any AI calls
- _parse_offline_plan() validates strictly: task_id must be in the
  project's valid ID set, priorities normalised, unknown action types
  silently skipped, observations and actions each capped at 5
- _run_offline_agent() retries up to 3 times on JSON parse failure,
  appending a corrective suffix that explains the error each time
- Same _confirm_write() guard used by Anthropic path: dry-run / auto-yes
  / interactive prompt all work identically

728 tests (+66 vs M17), 0 warnings.

Prove perfect matchings double factorial formula (decompPairing bijection)

Complete the proof that |Pairing(2n)| = (2n-1)!! by proving both
decompPairing_injective and decompPairing_surjective, establishing
a bijection between Pairing(2n+2) and Fin(2n+1) × Pairing(2n).

Key components:
- expandedPairing: constructs pairing from partner choice + smaller pairing
- expand/compress round trips for the bijection
- compressFinTotal_injective_valid for the injectivity argument
- Wire up gap_pairing_card in FeynmanGraphs.lean

execution paths, relayed via IPC from worker to parent, collected in an EventBus

callback is optional so CLI usage is unchanged.

when cached entities are accessed across different async sessions.

commit.message:
Fix: _go_with_retry() — JLINKARM_Go() 반환값 체크 및 재시도

JLINKARM_Go()는 실패 시 Python exception이 아닌 음수 반환값을 돌려준다.
기존 코드의 try/except는 실패를 감지하지 못해 CPU가 halt 상태로 방치됨.

SWD 환경에서 NVMe DMA 처리 중 CPU 클럭 게이팅으로 Go()가 일시 실패하는 현상
(로그: "Could not start CPU core. (ErrorCode: -1)")을 재시도로 복구한다.

- _go_with_retry(): 최대 5회, 50ms 간격 재시도 + IsHalted() 실제 running 확인
- _read_pc() finally에 적용
- _ensure_running()에 적용

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Refresh badges after admin add/remove, fix pending totals, fix Invalid Date

Admin portal — auto-refresh registration panel:
- Extracted content-loading from toggleEventRegistrations into
  refreshEventRegistrations(eventId) so it can be called without toggling
- adminAddRegStudent, adminAddRegObserver, adminRemoveRegStudent, and
  adminRemoveRegObserver all call refreshEventRegistrations after success
  so payment badges, pending totals, and student lists update immediately

Admin registrations API — include pending state:
- GET /admin/events/{event_id}/registrations now returns pending_student_ids
  and pending_amount (calculated from event pricing) per registration

paymentBadge — show outstanding balance:
- When a registration has pending unpaid dancers/observers, the badge now
  appends a red "⚡ $X.XX due" badge next to the Paid/Admin Paid/Free status

Summary pills — Balance Due pill:
- Added red "⚡ N Balance Due · $X.XX" pill to the event registrations
  header summarising all outstanding pending balances across registrations

Fix: "Invalid Date" in studio transactions modal:
- event_date from the backend may already contain a time component; stripping
  at 'T' before appending 'T12:00:00' prevents the double-time-suffix that
  caused Invalid Date in some browsers

commit.message:
feat: LBD synthesis engine + 3-message Telegram + feedback loop

Three major upgrades:

1. LBD synthesis (discoverer.py + summarizer.py)
   - TF-IDF vectorizes all filtered articles, finds pairs in
     goldilocks zone (0.15–0.45 cosine similarity) from different
     publications — the Swanson ABC model applied to GTM content
   - Groq synthesizes bridge concepts between pairs, outputs 3
     novel Twitter posts (not summaries)

2. 3 separate Telegram messages (telegram.py)
   - Parses ---POST N--- delimiters, sends each as its own message
   - Each message has 👍/👎 inline keyboard buttons
   - Saves message_log.json with message_id → snippet mapping

3. Feedback loop (feedback.py)
   - Next day's cron polls getUpdates for callback_queries
   - Matches clicks to posts, updates preferences.json
   - Liked/disliked snippets injected into Groq prompt for
     personalization over time

New files: discoverer.py, feedback.py
Updated: summarizer.py, telegram.py, main.py, requirements.txt

Increase gunicorn worker timeout to 180s for Anthropic API calls

PDF document analysis via the Claude API can take 60-120 seconds,
which exceeds gunicorn's default 30s worker timeout. This was causing
500 Internal Server Errors on the /api/score proxy endpoint.
